title: lupinus albus subsp. graecus
intro: Lupinus albus subsp. graecus, also known as the Greek white lupin, is a subspecies of the white lupin plant (Lupinus albus), a legume native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of isoflavones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Lupinus albus subsp. graecus include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving blood sugar control, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or supplements, and are often used to promote cardiovascular health and support the immune system.
